articles.title: Calls for Crackdown on HMOs in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Residents are urging authorities to take action against Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) in Rochdale, citing concerns over community impact and living conditions.
articles.content_html:
Residents in Rochdale are calling for a crackdown on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) due to concerns about their impact on local communities. Complaints regarding overcrowding and poor living conditions in some properties have been rising.

Local campaigners argue that the proliferation of HMOs is affecting the quality of life for long-term residents, with issues such as noise and litter being significant problems associated with these properties.

The council has been urged to review regulations surrounding HMOs to ensure they meet appropriate standards and do not disrupt neighbourhoods.

As discussions continue, the local authority is expected to consider the implications of HMOs on community wellbeing.

articles.title: Rochdale Masonic Hall Celebrates Centenary
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Masonic Hall marks its centenary with a special ceremony, including a re-enactment of the foundation stone laying.
articles.content_html:
A ceremony to celebrate the centenary of Rochdale Masonic Hall took place recently, featuring a re-enactment of the laying of the foundation stone.

The event was organised by the Provincial Grand Lodge of East Lancashire, highlighting the historical significance of the hall in the community.

Attendees were able to witness the traditional ceremony, which included an explanation of its importance and the rituals involved.

The centenary celebration reflects the hall's long-standing presence and role within Rochdale's social fabric.

articles.title: Rock Choir Launches New Group in Rochdale with Free Taster Sessions
articles.excerpt: Rock Choir has launched a new group in Rochdale, offering free taster sessions for those interested in joining. The initiative aims to bring together local residents through music.
articles.content_html:
Rock Choir has opened a new group in Rochdale, inviting residents to participate in free taster sessions. The sessions are designed to introduce potential members to the choir's activities and community.

Participants will have the opportunity to experience the benefits of singing and meet other local residents. The sessions are open to all, regardless of singing ability.

The new group aims to foster a sense of community and encourage more people to engage with music. Rock Choir is known for its inclusive approach, welcoming individuals of all backgrounds.

Those interested in joining can attend the free sessions, which will provide a glimpse into the choir's regular activities and performances.

articles.title: Missing Cat Gene Last Seen in Falinge Area of Rochdale
articles.excerpt: A grey and white cat named Gene has gone missing from the Falinge area of Rochdale. He is microchipped but not wearing a collar.
articles.content_html:
A cat named Gene has been reported missing from the Falinge area of Rochdale. The grey and white cat was last seen a couple of days ago.

Gene is microchipped but is not wearing a collar. He is known to be an outdoor cat, often coming and going as he pleases.

His owner has expressed concern, noting that it is unusual for him not to return home. Anyone who may have seen Gene or has information about his whereabouts is encouraged to reach out.

The missing cat's last known location was near Wasilla Drive and Clark Street in Rochdale. Residents in the area are asked to keep an eye out for him.

articles.title: 194kg of Produce Transferred to Rochdale Food Bank Following Pantry Closure
articles.excerpt: Following the closure of Irene’s Pantry, 194kg of produce was transferred to Rochdale Food Bank by local residents. The community effort was acknowledged at St James.
articles.content_html:
A group of residents transferred 194kg of produce to Rochdale Food Bank following the closure of Irene’s Pantry. The donation was made by Anne, Peter and Walter, who facilitated the transfer.

The food was hosted at St James, where the community effort was appreciated. Those involved in the operation of the Food Bank were thanked for their contributions.

This initiative highlights the ongoing support for local food banks, which play a crucial role in assisting those in need within the community.

The closure of Irene’s Pantry has prompted local residents to step in and ensure that surplus food is redirected to those who require it.

articles.title: Albion Inn raises £1,090 for Rochdale MS Society
articles.excerpt: The Albion Inn in Shawclough has raised £1,090 for the Rochdale branch of the MS Society following a community charity event.
articles.summary: Customers, organisers and supporters at the Albion Inn came together to raise £1,090 for MS Society Rochdale, with the pub thanking Natasha Jones and Craig Jones for coordinating the event and donations.
articles.content_html:
The Albion Inn in Shawclough has raised £1,090 for the Rochdale branch of the MS Society following a community charity event.

The pub announced the final total after donations and fundraising activities were brought together, thanking local residents and supporters for helping make the event a success.

Community effort raises four-figure total

The money was raised through a team effort involving customers, organisers and members of the wider community.

The Albion Inn gave special thanks to Natasha Jones and Craig Jones for organising the event programme and coordinating donations.

“Thank you once again to the whole community for your efforts. Great team effort by all.”

Supporting people affected by multiple sclerosis

The £1,090 donation will support MS Society Rochdale and its work with people affected by multiple sclerosis.

Multiple sclerosis is a condition affecting the brain and spinal cord and can cause a wide range of symptoms, including problems with movement, balance, vision and fatigue.

Local MS Society groups can provide information, social support and practical help for people living with the condition, as well as their families and carers.

Thanks to everyone who contributed

The pub thanked everyone who donated, attended or helped organise the fundraising effort.

The presentation image records the donation as £1,090 and is dated Saturday 11 July 2026.

This report is based on a public update and fundraising presentation image published by the Albion Inn following its charity event for MS Society Rochdale.

articles.title: Julie Murphy Appointed Permanent Chief Executive of Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Julie Murphy has been appointed as the permanent chief executive of Rochdale, following her interim role in the position.
articles.content_html:
Julie Murphy has been named the permanent chief executive of Rochdale. She previously served as the interim chief executive and has now officially taken on the role.

Murphy's appointment follows a period of leadership transition within the council. Her experience in local government is expected to support the ongoing development of services in the area.

The council has expressed confidence in Murphy's ability to lead and implement key initiatives for the community. Her focus will be on enhancing local services and engaging with residents.

This appointment is likely to bring continuity to the leadership of Rochdale, as the council aims to address various challenges and opportunities in the coming years.

articles.title: Rochdale Youth Service Hosts Annual Youth Pride in the Park Event
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Youth Service's annual Youth Pride in the Park event attracted over 250 young attendees at Queen’s Park, Heywood, last week, featuring music, games, and activities.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Youth Service held its annual Youth Pride in the Park event at Queen’s Park, Heywood, last week. The event saw more than 250 young people in attendance, enjoying a range of activities.

Participants enjoyed music, games, and various other activities throughout the evening, which was marked by pleasant weather. The event provided a vibrant atmosphere for attendees to engage with one another.

The event aims to celebrate diversity and promote inclusivity among young people in the community. It serves as an important platform for fostering connections and support among attendees.

This gathering allowed young people to connect and engage in a supportive environment, reinforcing the values of acceptance and community spirit.

articles.title: Rochdale Borough Noticeboard Launches on Facebook
articles.excerpt: Recent updates from the Rochdale Borough Noticeboard include a series of community announcements and events. The posts cover a range of topics relevant to local residents, including public meetings and local initiatives. One announcement highlights an upcoming community event aimed at fostering local engagement. Furthe
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 19 July 2026 at 22:36. 5 source updates have been combined into this article.

Recent updates from the Rochdale Borough Noticeboard include a series of community announcements and events. The posts cover a range of topics relevant to local residents, including public meetings and local initiatives. One announcement highlights an upcoming community event aimed at fostering local engagement. Further details about the event, including…

Latest update

A Facebook page called Rochdale Borough Noticeboard has been launched to connect local businesses and residents. The page serves as a platform for sharing information related to local commerce and community activities.

The noticeboard aims to facilitate engagement by promoting services and events happening in the area. Users can find updates and posts from various local enterprises.

This initiative is expected to enhance community interaction and support local businesses by providing a space for them to share their offerings.

For more information, visit the Rochdale Borough Noticeboard on Facebook.

Earlier developments

Recent updates from the Rochdale Borough Noticeboard include a series of community announcements and events. The posts cover a range of topics relevant to local residents, including public meetings and local initiatives.

One announcement highlights an upcoming community event aimed at fostering local engagement. Further details about the event, including the date and location, are expected to be shared soon.

In addition, the noticeboard features updates on local services and resources available to residents. This includes information on support services and community resources that may be beneficial.

Residents are encouraged to check the noticeboard regularly for the latest information and updates pertinent to the Rochdale area.

Update timeline

  • 19 July 2026, 22:36: Rochdale Borough Noticeboard Launches on Facebook
  • 17 July 2026, 10:15: Rochdale Borough Noticeboard Updates

articles.title: Mayoral candidate raises alarm over intoxicated children and predatory behaviour in Manchester
articles.excerpt: Greater Manchester mayoral candidate Marlon Scott West has raised serious concerns after a late-night visit to Piccadilly Gardens revealed children appearing intoxicated, suspected drug use, anti-social behaviour and inappropriate approaches towards women.
articles.summary: Marlon Scott West and Montgomery Toms visited Piccadilly Gardens at night and highlighted scenes involving young people apparently under the influence of alcohol or drugs, alongside behaviour by groups of men which campaigners described as predatory and potentially criminal.
articles.content_html:
Greater Manchester mayoral candidate Marlon Scott West has raised serious concerns about public safety after witnessing troubling scenes during a late-night visit to Piccadilly Gardens.

West visited the busy Manchester city-centre location alongside campaigner Montgomery Toms to examine the conditions faced by residents, visitors and commuters after dark.

The footage recorded during the visit showed young people who appeared to be children visibly affected by alcohol or other substances, alongside suspected public drug use, disorder and widespread anti-social behaviour.

Children appeared intoxicated in public

One of the most concerning aspects of the visit was the presence of young people who appeared to be under the age of 18 and heavily intoxicated.

The campaigners questioned why children were able to remain in such a vulnerable condition in a major public space without apparent intervention from responsible adults or public services.

They argued that visibly intoxicated children in a city-centre environment could be at significant risk of exploitation, violence, accidental injury or becoming involved in criminal activity.

Inappropriate and predatory behaviour towards women

The visit also highlighted repeated approaches towards women by groups of men, with behaviour described by those present as inappropriate, intimidating and predatory.

Campaigners said some of the conduct witnessed went beyond behaviour that could simply be described as uncomfortable and appeared to border on criminal harassment or public-order offending.

The men involved were described by the campaigners as foreign nationals. Rochdale Daily has not independently established the identity, nationality or immigration status of any individual shown.

No allegation of a specific criminal offence should be inferred against an identifiable person unless confirmed following an investigation by Greater Manchester Police.

Suspected drug use and alcohol-related disorder

West and Toms also reported witnessing suspected drug use, people heavily under the influence of alcohol and behaviour that disrupted the safety and accessibility of the area.

They said the conditions were unacceptable for one of Greater Manchester’s most prominent public spaces and transport hubs.

Piccadilly Gardens is used by thousands of commuters and visitors each day, including people travelling into Manchester from Rochdale, Middleton, Heywood and the Pennines.

Calls for intervention and stronger enforcement

West said the scenes demonstrated a need for more visible policing, stronger enforcement and clearer intervention where children, vulnerable people or women may be at risk.

The campaign also called for greater scrutiny of how anti-social behaviour, suspected drug activity, public intoxication and threatening conduct are managed in Manchester city centre.

Campaigners argued that support services for addiction, homelessness and vulnerable young people must operate alongside effective enforcement against intimidation, harassment and criminal behaviour.

Public safety becomes mayoral election issue

The visit forms part of West’s campaign to become the next Mayor of Greater Manchester.

Policing, safeguarding, public-order enforcement and the condition of town and city centres are expected to remain prominent issues during the mayoral election campaign.

West said residents should be able to travel through Greater Manchester, use public transport and enjoy the city’s public spaces without encountering intoxicated children, open drug use, predatory behaviour or serious disorder.

This report is based on footage and campaign material supplied following a late-night visit by Marlon Scott West and Montgomery Toms to Piccadilly Gardens. Descriptions of intoxication, suspected drug use, predatory conduct and potentially criminal behaviour reflect what was shown in the footage and the observations made by those present. Rochdale Daily has not independently confirmed the identity, age, nationality, immigration status or criminal conduct of any identifiable individual.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article reports observations made during a political campaign visit. Rochdale Daily has not independently established the identity, age, nationality, immigration status or criminal conduct of any person shown. References to suspected drug use, intoxication, predatory behaviour and conduct bordering on criminality do not amount to a finding of guilt. No wrongdoing should be inferred against an identifiable individual unless confirmed by police or a court.
articles.right_to_reply: Marlon Scott West, Montgomery Toms, Manchester City Council, Greater Manchester Police and anyone materially affected by this report may submit a response or request a factual correction by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: Search Underway for Missing Dog in Castleton
articles.excerpt: A 12-year-old Maltese dog has gone missing from Thornham New Road in Castleton, prompting a search by the family and local community.
articles.content_html:
A 12-year-old Maltese dog has gone missing from Thornham New Road in Castleton, Rochdale. The dog was last seen in the area, and her family is urging residents to check their sheds and gardens for any sign of her.

The family has expressed their desire to have the dog returned home safely. They have shared information about the dog's disappearance on social media to reach a wider audience.

Local residents are encouraged to keep an eye out and report any sightings to the family. The community's support could be vital in locating the missing pet.

Anyone with information regarding the dog's whereabouts is asked to contact the family directly.

articles.title: Free Vacation Bible Camp to be Held at Fellowship Bible Church in Castleton
articles.excerpt: Fellowship Bible Church in Castleton will host a free Vacation Bible Camp, providing local children with an opportunity for summer engagement and learning.
articles.content_html:
Fellowship Bible Church in Castleton will host a free Vacation Bible Camp this summer. The event aims to provide children in the local community with an engaging and educational experience.

The camp will include various activities designed to promote learning and fellowship among participants. It is open to children of all ages, encouraging families to take part in the community event.

Details regarding the specific dates and times of the camp will be announced soon, allowing families to plan accordingly. The church invites all interested families to participate in this opportunity.

Anyone with questions about the camp can contact Fellowship Bible Church directly for more information.

articles.title: Community protest planned in Middleton town centre next month
articles.excerpt: Organisers have announced plans for a public protest in Middleton on 8 August, inviting residents to attend and share their views on issues including illegal immigration and community concerns.
articles.summary: A demonstration has been announced for Middleton Gardens on Saturday 8 August. Organisers say the event will feature guest speakers and provide an opportunity for people to express their views on immigration and related issues.
articles.content_html:
A public protest has been scheduled to take place in Middleton town centre next month.

Organisers have announced that the event will begin at 1pm on Saturday 8 August at Middleton Gardens and are encouraging residents who wish to attend to do so peacefully and respectfully.

The demonstration is being promoted as a protest against illegal immigration and is expected to include speeches from invited guests and community campaigners.

Event details announced

According to promotional material shared online, the gathering will take place at Middleton Gardens, with attendees invited to bring flags and take part in the demonstration.

Named speakers currently include Ashlea Simon, Jamie Thomason and PagUK Billy, with organisers indicating that additional speakers may be announced closer to the event.

The event is being organised by campaigner Jamie Thomason, who has invited members of the public wishing to speak to contact him directly.

Community response expected

Public demonstrations often attract a range of opinions and viewpoints from residents. Organisers say the event is intended to provide a platform for people to voice concerns and discuss issues they believe affect local communities.

As with all public gatherings, attendees are expected to comply with relevant laws and any conditions imposed by local authorities or police to ensure the safety of participants and the wider public.

Residents advised to plan ahead

People visiting Middleton town centre on the day of the protest are advised to be aware of the planned gathering, which may lead to increased footfall in and around Middleton Gardens during the afternoon.

This report is based on promotional material and public social media posts advertising the event. Rochdale Daily is reporting the planned gathering as a matter of public interest and does not endorse any political organisation, campaign group or viewpoint associated with the event.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article reports the existence of a planned public demonstration. The views expressed by organisers, speakers or attendees are their own. Attendance at any public event should be peaceful and in accordance with applicable laws and public-order requirements.

This initiative may enhance community awareness of housing services available in Rochdale and encourage collaboration between residents and the organisation.

The strategy aims to address staffing challenges and ensure that healthcare providers can meet the growing demands of the community.

Details regarding specific roles and the timeline for implementation have not yet been disclosed. Further updates will be provided as the strategy develops.

articles.title: Greater Manchester Leads in Civil Society Collaboration
articles.excerpt: Greater Manchester is being recognised for its leadership in civil society collaboration, according to a report by Action Together. The initiative aims to foster partnerships among community organisations to enhance social impact across the region.
articles.content_html:
Greater Manchester is being recognised for its leadership in civil society collaboration, according to a report by Action Together. The initiative aims to foster partnerships among community organisations to enhance social impact across the region.

The report outlines successful projects that have emerged from this collaborative approach, showcasing how local groups are working together to address community needs effectively.

Action Together emphasises the importance of these partnerships in creating a more resilient environment for residents.

This recognition may inspire more community organisations in Rochdale and beyond to collaborate, potentially leading to improved services for local residents.

articles.title: Twelve Green Spaces in Heywood Celebrate Green Flag Award Success
articles.excerpt: Twelve green spaces across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines have received the Green Flag Award for another year, recognising the efforts of local teams and volunteers.
articles.content_html:
Twelve green spaces across Heywood, Rochdale, Middleton and the Pennines are celebrating another year of success in the Green Flag Award scheme. This accolade highlights the quality and management of these green spaces, which are vital to the local community.

The Green Flag Award is a national standard for parks and green spaces, recognising well-managed sites that meet specific criteria. The award is a testament to the hard work of local teams and volunteers who dedicate their time to maintaining these areas throughout the year.

The success of these green spaces contributes positively to the local environment and community well-being, providing residents with accessible areas for recreation and relaxation.

This achievement reflects the ongoing commitment to enhancing the quality of life in the region, and it is hoped that the recognition will encourage further community involvement in maintaining and improving local green spaces.
